CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-25-2019 Continued To Feb-04-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce A Settlement Agreement And Enter Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-21-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew B. Dorr, Esq. of Clerkin Sinclair & Mahfouz LLP, T:619.308.6550, for plaintiff/moving party, on Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: Plaintiff argued the tentative ruling. The terms of the settlement was discussed. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Enforce A Settlement Agreement And Enter Judgment.

Plaintiff State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company's motion to enforce settlement agreement and enter judgment is denied. On its face, the motion seeks enforcement of an illegal penalty. A settlement agreement that allows a plaintiff to file a stipulation for entry of judgment upon default by the defendant will not be enforced if the amount of the judgment constitutes an unenforceable penalty because it bears no reasonable relationship to the range of actual damages the parties could have anticipated would flow from a breach of their settlement agreement. (E.g., Red & White Distribution, LLC v. Osteroid Enterprises, LLC (2019) 38 Cal.App.5th 582, 588-591; Greentree Financial Group, Inc. v. Execute Sports, Inc. (2008) 163 Cal.App.4th 495, 500-501.)

Here, the settlement agreement required Defendant Allen Cris Santo Enriquez to pay $4,975.00 by a $75.00 down payment upon the signing of the agreement, followed by monthly installments of $75.00, $125.00, and $175.00 at various time periods until the $4,975.00 was paid in full. The agreement provides that if Defendant is in default, judgment shall be entered in the amount of $11,103.20 - more than twice the amount Defendant was otherwise required to pay in settlement - plus interest, costs of suit and reasonable attorney's fees, minus amounts paid by Defendant. That provision constitutes an unenforceable penalty. (See Red & White Distribution, LLC, 38 Cal.App.5th at 588-589 [stipulated judgment for $2.8 million was unenforceable penalty because defendant only owed $2.1 million under the settlement agreement]; Vivatech International, Inc. v. Sporn (2017) 16 Cal.App.5th 796, 808-812 [stipulated judgment for more than $300,000 for failure to pay $75,000 settlement payment was unenforceable penalty]; Purcell v. Schweitzer (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 969, 974-976 [stipulated judgment for nearly $60,000 constituted illegal penalty for breach of settlement agreement requiring payment of $38,000); Greentree, 163 Cal.App.4th at 498-500 [$61,232.50 judgment, entered after defendant failed to make $15,000 installment payment out of $20,000 total settlement payment, was "more than triple the amount for which the parties agreed to settle the case" and constituted an illegal penalty]; Sybron Corp. v. Clark Hosp. Supply Corp. (1978) 76 Cal.App.3d 896, 903 [$100,000 stipulated judgment, entered after buyers defaulted on installment payments totaling $30,000 out of a settlement agreement to pay $72,000, could not be enforced].)

The Court believes that judgment properly may be entered against Defendant in the amount of $3,325.00 (representing the settlement amount of $4,975.00, minus the $1,650.00 paid by Defendant), plus interest on that amount at the legal rate of 7% from the date of default, plus costs of suit and reasonable attorney's fees. (See Red & White Distribution, LLC, 38 Cal.App.5th at 590 [reversing and remanding to trial court with directions to reduce judgment to the amount specified in the settlement agreement, less agreed upon payments, plus interest];Greentree, 163 Cal.App.4th at 498, 503 [same].) If plaintiff agrees, it may submit a revised proposed order and judgment together with a sufficient showing of the accrued interest, costs, and fees.

Counsel for plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order that repeats verbatim the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
